database.name: vine_marketplace
database.description: Vine is a commercial real estate lending marketplace that connects developers, lenders, and brokers. It manages the full deal lifecycle: from deal origination and document collection through underwriting, collateral assessment, and closing.
database.core_workflow: Developer submits a Project → Deal is created → Lenders collaborate → Documents and financials collected → Collateral assessed → Deal closes

Organizations.description: Companies participating in the marketplace — developers (borrowers), lenders, brokers, and administrators. The central entity most other records link to.

columns.organizationType_id: FK to OrganizationTypes — defines whether this org is a lender, developer, broker, etc.
columns.createdByOrganization_id: Self-referential FK — which org created/invited this org

common_queries: SELECT * FROM Organizations WHERE organizationType_id = (SELECT id FROM OrganizationTypes WHERE name = 'lender') LIMIT 20

SELECT o.id, ot.name as type, COUNT(d.id) as deal_count FROM Organizations o JOIN OrganizationTypes ot ON o.organizationType_id = ot.id LEFT JOIN Deals d ON d.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id LIMIT 20

OrganizationTypes.description: Lookup table defining the role of an organization: lender, developer, broker, admin, etc.

Deals.description: Loan/deal applications on the platform. A Deal represents a financing request by an organization for a Project. It has a type (DealTypes), belongs to an organization, and is linked to a project.

columns.name: Human-readable deal name
columns.organization_id: FK to Organizations — the originating/borrower organization
columns.project_id: FK to Projects — the underlying real estate project
columns.dealType_id: FK to DealTypes — type of financing (bridge, construction, etc.)

common_queries: SELECT d.name, ot.name as org_type, dt.name as deal_type, d.createdAt FROM Deals d JOIN Organizations o ON d.organization_id = o.id JOIN OrganizationTypes ot ON o.organizationType_id = ot.id JOIN DealTypes dt ON d.dealType_id = dt.id ORDER BY d.createdAt DESC LIMIT 20
SELECT COUNT(*) as total_deals, dt.name as deal_type FROM Deals d JOIN DealTypes dt ON d.dealType_id = dt.id GROUP BY dt.id

DealTypes.description: Lookup table for types of financing deals — e.g. bridge loan, construction loan, permanent financing.

Projects.description: Real estate projects underlying deals. A project represents a physical property or development. Multiple deals can exist for the same project.

columns.developer_id: FK to Developers — the developer responsible for this project

common_queries: SELECT p.id, COUNT(d.id) as deal_count FROM Projects p LEFT JOIN Deals d ON d.project_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id LIMIT 20

Users.description: Authenticated platform users. Each user belongs to exactly one organization. Users have preferences (JSON) for UI settings like language, date format, currency.

columns.refreshToken: JWT refresh token — SENSITIVE, do not expose
columns.preferences: JSON blob — language, dateLocale, contractDateFormat, currencySymbol, notifications config
columns.uiState: JSON blob — last UI state for rememberUiState feature
columns.organization_id: FK to Organizations — which company this user belongs to

common_queries: SELECT u.id, u.createdAt, o.id as org_id FROM Users u JOIN Organizations o ON u.organization_id = o.id LIMIT 20

Contacts.description: External contacts associated with organizations — people who are not platform users but are tracked for relationship management (e.g. attorneys, appraisers, referrals).

columns.organization_id: FK to Organizations — which org owns this contact
columns.user_id: FK to Users — platform user who manages this contact (nullable)

Documents.description: Files uploaded to the platform — financial statements, appraisals, contracts, title reports. Linked to projects and uploaded by users.

columns.project_id: FK to Projects — which project this document belongs to
columns.user_id: FK to Users — who uploaded the document

Collateral.description: Collateral assets securing a deal — real estate properties, equipment, or other assets pledged by the borrower. A deal can have multiple collateral items.

columns.creating_entity_id: FK to Organizations — which org created this collateral record

Deal_X_Collateral.description: Junction table linking deals to their collateral assets (many-to-many).

Facts.description: Structured financial and property data points collected during underwriting. Each Fact is a single data value (e.g. NOI, LTV, appraised value) defined by a FactDefinition.

columns.collateral_record_id: FK to Collateral — which asset this fact describes

FactDefinitions.description: Schema/template definitions for the fact data model — defines what financial metrics are tracked (e.g. 'Net Operating Income', 'Loan-to-Value Ratio').

PfsSheets.description: Personal Financial Statements — structured financial summaries for borrowers. Contains balance sheet and income data used in underwriting.

ChatConversations.description: Messaging threads attached to deals between deal participants (lenders, developers, brokers).

columns.deal_id: FK to Deals — which deal this conversation is about

DataRooms.description: Virtual data rooms for secure document sharing during due diligence. Linked to projects and hosted via a DataRoomProvider (e.g. Box, Dropbox).

BankerLists.description: Curated lists of organizations or projects maintained by a user — essentially a lender's watchlist or pipeline tracker.

columns.user_id: FK to Users — who owns this list

Roles.description: Permission roles on the platform — defines what actions users of an organization type can perform.

Permissions.description: Granular permissions that can be assigned to roles.

Organization_Collaborates_Deals.description: Junction table — which organizations have access/collaboration rights on which deals. Enables multi-party deal rooms.

MilestoneLists.description: Checklists of milestones/tasks for a deal — tracks deal progress through closing stages.

Histories.description: Audit trail of actions taken on projects — tracks changes and activity for compliance and review.

columns.user_id: FK to Users — who performed the action

NaicsCategories.description: NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) codes — used to classify the industry type of a borrower or project.

RmaAverages.description: RMA (Risk Management Association) industry average benchmarks — used to compare borrower financials against industry standards during underwriting.

QuickSightDashboards.description: AWS QuickSight embedded analytics dashboards — configuration for which dashboards are available to which roles/org types.

ReportDefinitions.description: Definitions for custom reports available on the platform.

FinanceSheetTemplates.description: Templates for financial statement collection forms used during underwriting.

ViewDefinitions.description: UI view configuration definitions — defines what columns and filters appear in list views for different org types.

ScheduledJobs.description: Background job scheduling records — tracks recurring automated tasks.

common_analytics.name: Deals by organization
common_analytics.sql: SELECT o.id, COUNT(d.id) as deal_count FROM Organizations o LEFT JOIN Deals d ON d.organization_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id ORDER BY deal_count DESC LIMIT 20

common_analytics.name: Deals by type
common_analytics.description: Distribution of deals across financing types
common_analytics.sql: SELECT dt.name as deal_type, COUNT(d.id) as count FROM Deals d JOIN DealTypes dt ON d.dealType_id = dt.id GROUP BY dt.id ORDER BY count DESC

common_analytics.name: Recent deals
common_analytics.description: Latest deals created on the platform
common_analytics.sql: SELECT d.id, d.name, d.createdAt, dt.name as type FROM Deals d JOIN DealTypes dt ON d.dealType_id = dt.id ORDER BY d.createdAt DESC LIMIT 20

common_analytics.name: Organizations by type
common_analytics.description: Count of organizations per type (lender, developer, broker)
common_analytics.sql: SELECT ot.name as org_type, COUNT(o.id) as count FROM Organizations o JOIN OrganizationTypes ot ON o.organizationType_id = ot.id GROUP BY ot.id ORDER BY count DESC

common_analytics.name: Active collaborations
common_analytics.sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(ocd.organization_id) as collaborators FROM Deals d JOIN Organization_Collaborates_Deals ocd ON d.id = ocd.deal_id GROUP BY d.id ORDER BY collaborators DESC LIMIT 20
